artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a central USI Desk page for ChatGPT and generative AI, official June 2025 recommendations for students and teaching staff, and official USI news summaries. The 17 June 2025 thesis news item was treated as limited context because the recommendations PDFs and central Desk page provide stronger source evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for the USI student community
Generative AI in education: Recommendations for USI teaching staff

artifacts.claimText: USI maintains a public generative-AI guidance hub and June 2025 recommendations for both students and teaching staff; the Desk page says those recommendations expand and replace earlier 2023 communications.
USI's general rule permits use of generative AI tools unless a specific activity explicitly prohibits them, and says use must be correctly acknowledged.

USI student recommendations say students must follow teachers' instructions for GenAI use in written work; complete drafting of exam papers, dissertations, or final papers with GenAI is generally not permitted, and unauthorised use is academic fraud subject to study-regulation penalties.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for the USI student community

artifacts.claimText: USI teaching-staff recommendations advise teachers to clearly communicate what students should, can, and cannot do with GenAI tools for course learning activities and to include relevant information in the course syllabus.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for USI teaching staff

artifacts.claimText: USI recommendations say GenAI use should account for data protection and that confidential documents and sensitive data should not be shared with GenAI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for the USI student community

artifacts.claimText: USI teaching-staff recommendations strongly discourage reliance on tools designed to detect GenAI-generated text, saying suspected misuse should follow normal misconduct procedures defined in faculty study regulations.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for USI teaching staff

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: In June 2025, the Recommendations on the use of generative AI in education for USI's faculty and students have been published. These documents expand and replace previous communications of 2023.
Building on this, practical recommendations have been developed for both teaching staff and students.

As a general rule the use of generative AI tools is thus permitted, unless explicitly prohibited for a specific activity (e.g. exam, assignment, specific tasks, etc.). However, their use must always be correctly acknowledged.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for the USI student community
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The use of Gen AI is encouraged, unless for specific activities where it is explicitly prohibited. It is the responsibility of the teachers and the students to explicitly agree on the conditions of use of the Gen AI within courses and for writing dissertations, papers, etc.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for the USI student community

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for the USI student community
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: In general, when writing exam papers, final papers, theses, etc., students are required to strictly follow their teachers' instructions regarding the use of Gen AI tools. As a general rule, the use of Gen AI tools for the complete drafting of exam papers, dissertations, final papers, etc. is not permitted.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 2.2, Using generative AI for writing assignments, dissertations, final papers, etc.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for the USI student community

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for the USI student community
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It is their responsibility to follow the instructions received from their teachers: unauthorised use of Gen AI tools is considered academic fraud and, as such, is subject to the penalties provided for in the respective study regulations.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for the USI student community

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for USI teaching staff
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Teachers are advised to clearly communicate at the beginning and during the course what students should /can /cannot do with Gen AI tools for learning activities related to their course. For this purpose, it is recommended to include a section in the course syllabus summarising the relevant information.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 2.2, Use of Gen AI during the course

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for USI teaching staff

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for the USI student community
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Gen AI must be used critically, competently and responsibly, paying particular attention to the trustworthiness of the answers provided, data protection, environmental sustainability aspects, and ensuring that confidential documents and sensitive data are not shared.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for the USI student community

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Users are expected to critically review AI supported outputs, ensure their accuracy, and comply with ethical standards, data protection, and copyright regulations.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for USI teaching staff
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: However, it is strongly discouraged to rely on tools designed to detect text generated with the help of Gen AI (e.g. ChatGPTzero and similar). These tools are still unreliable and of little use, as they only provide a statistical estimate without offering any definite proof of the actual use of such tools.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 2.4, Use of Gen AI for dissertation work, final papers, delivery of exam papers, etc.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI in education: Recommendations for USI teaching staff

artifacts.notes: Approved as a source-status claim because official USI pages directly identify the current GenAI hub and June 2025 recommendation documents.
Claim follows the wording of the official Desk page and student recommendations without asserting unconditional permission.
Approved as a narrow student academic-integrity claim tied to the official student recommendation PDF.
Approved as teaching guidance; does not convert recommendations into a broader formal policy beyond course-level communication advice.
Approved as a privacy/confidentiality caution because the source text explicitly references data protection, confidential documents, and sensitive data.
Approved because the teaching-staff recommendations directly discourage reliance on AI-text detectors and point suspected misuse to normal misconduct procedures.

artifacts.title: USI - Università della Svizzera italiana GenAI Guidance Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: The crawl found official USI generative-AI guidance: a central Desk page and June 2025 recommendations for students and teaching staff. Claims are staged narrowly around permitted use with explicit prohibitions and acknowledgement, student writing and academic-fraud boundaries, teaching communication duties, privacy/confidentiality cautions, and AI-detector unreliability.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged claims are based on English official pages and PDFs found during this crawl; they should not be treated as human-reviewed canonical policy.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
